differentials.name: Hypergonadotropic hypogonadism =  ovarian tissue fails to respond to endogenous gonadotropins
differentials.raw: Hypergonadotropic hypogonadism =  ovarian tissue fails to respond to endogenous gonadotropins

differentials.name: high LH + FSH levels
differentials.raw: high LH + FSH levels (a)  abnormal karyotype: Turner syndrome, XY gonadal dysgenesis (b)  irradiation, chemotherapy, autoimmune disease (eg, autoimmune oophoritis)

differentials.name: Testicular feminization = male intersex = male pseudohermaphroditism ← end- organ insensitivity to testosterone
differentials.raw: Testicular feminization = male intersex = male pseudohermaphroditism ← end- organ insensitivity to testosterone

differentials.name: Androgen-producing virilizing tumor of adolescent ovary ← usually Sertoli-Leydig cell tumor
differentials.raw: Androgen-producing virilizing tumor of adolescent ovary ← usually Sertoli-Leydig cell tumor

differentials.name: Estrogen-producing ovarian tumor with disruption of menstrual cycle: granulosa cell tumor, thecoma
differentials.raw: Estrogen-producing ovarian tumor with disruption of menstrual cycle: granulosa cell tumor, thecoma

differentials.name: Transverse vaginal septum ›  in upper vagina
differentials.raw: Transverse vaginal septum ›  in upper vagina (45%) ›  in mid vagina (40%) ›  in lower vagina (15%)

differentials.name: Polycystic ovary syndrome (= Stein-Leventhal syndrome): most common cause of secondary amenorrhea
differentials.raw: Polycystic ovary syndrome (= Stein-Leventhal syndrome): most common cause of secondary amenorrhea

context: (b)  pituitary dysfunction: disruption of pituitary stalk from child abuse, head trauma √  absent uterus: √  normal fallopian tubes + ovaries Associated with:   unilateral renal abnormality (50%), skeletal abnormality (12%) √  small infantile uterus: √  unilateral adnexal mass √  normal uterus + unilateral ovarian tumor: √  hematometrocolpos: (a)  neonate = congenital uterovaginal obstruction √  pelviabdominal cystic mass with fluid-debris level in fetal US during 3rd trimester √  renal dysplasia / obstruction (b)  teenager √  hematometra √  bilateral ovarian enlargement:
